title: feat(goal-guard): infra-punt 3rd catch вҖ” block working around an infra wall, force upstream-fix
body: goal-guard gains a THIRD Stop-hook catch: infra-punt. When a reply reports an 'infra'/'мқён”„лқј' wall (keyword + a failure signal on the SAME line) AND works around it with a punt verb (мҡ°нҡҢВ·кІ©лҰ¬В·лҜёлЈёВ·fallbackВ·wrapperВ·workaroundвҖҰ) instead of fixing the cause, the stop is blocked with an upstream-fix directive (fix the cause in its canonical repo this session вҶ’ verify вҶ’ pr-cycle; no wall-covering / vendored-only / deferral). Per user request '''infra'/'мқён”„лқј' нӮӨмӣҢл“ң к°җм§ҖмӢң мқён”„лқј м§Ғм ‘ fix'. Over-fire is contained by a TRIPLE AND (punt verb whole-message + infra keyword + wall signal same-line), a line-scoped already-fixed negation ('мҲҳм • мҷ„лЈҢ/к·ёлҰ°' on the keyword line passes), quote/backtick meta-mention skip + kebab-slug ('infra-wall-noneval') lookaround exclusion, and reuse of hasSessionTerminalBlocker so a genuine external/approval/other-machine infra wall with 'ing next' passes on the first stop. 3rd catch reuses goal-guard's existing single-fire + wiring (hooks.json + setup.ts Stop[]) вҖ” no new surface wiring. 14/14 smoke PASS.

title: feat(goal-guard): session-terminal blocker exemption + quoted meta-mention skip for remnant scan
body: goal-guard remnant scan gains a session-terminal exemption + meta-mention skip. Two false-blocks fixed: (1) a legitimate session-terminal reply that reports 'мһ”м—¬' leftover work ALONGSIDE a CONCRETE blocker (CI/checks wait В· human approval/input В· external dep/service/API В· another machine, per commons session-terminal) AND names the resume point ('ing next') now passes on the FIRST stop instead of eating a block cycle вҖ” new BLOCKER_RE + ING_NEXT_RE + hasSessionTerminalBlocker(), AND-gated so a bare excuse ('лё”лЎңм»Ө: к·ңлӘЁк°Җ нҒј', no concrete noun / no resume point) can NOT slip through; excuse-shapes (sizeВ·rate-limit) stay with DEFER_RE which runs independently. (2) a reply DISCUSSING the keyword ('мһ”м—¬' wrapped in quotes/backticks) is meta-discussion, not live work вҖ” isQuotedMention() skips quote/backtick-wrapped occurrences so a reply about the guard no longer false-blocks. 13/13 smoke PASS.

title: fix(pool): pool on --timeout flag вҖ” unblock long remote jobs (was hard 120s SIGKILL)
body: pool on --timeout <sec> flag (default 120s В· 0=unbounded): the hardcoded 120s SSH cap SIGKILLed long remote builds/sweeps/heavy compute mid-run вҖ” defeating heavy-on-pool. Root cause pool.ts:362 timeoutMs:120_000. Verified aiden: --timeout 0 survives 125s job (past old wall), --timeout 5 cuts a 20s job at 5.27s, default preserved, prompt return post-completion. no keepalive needed (LANВ·execArgs timer was the wall, not idle-drop).

title: fix(git-add-guard): quote-aware нҶ нҒ¬лӮҳмқҙм Җ вҖ” мқёмҡ© мқёмһҗ мҶҚ мҳҲмӢң л¬ёкө¬ мҳӨнғҗ м ңкұ°(self-QA)
body: RC3 нӣ„мҶҚ self-QA fix: git-add-guard detectAddAllмқҙ stripQuotesлЎң мқёмҡ©л¶ҖнҳёлҘј м ңкұ°н•ҙ мқёмҡ© мқёмһҗ мҶҚ л¬ёмһҗм—ҙ(commit л©”мӢңм§ҖВ·ing/changelog л…ёнҠём—җ лӢҙкёҙ мҳҲмӢң л¬ёкө¬)к№Ңм§Җ лӘ…л № нҶ нҒ°мңјлЎң мҳӨмқёвҶ’м •мғҒ лӘ…л № мҳӨм°ЁлӢЁ(л°ңкІ¬ мҰүмӢң: мә нҺҳмқё ING л…ёнҠё мһ‘м„ұмқҙ м°ЁлӢЁлҗЁ). fix=blankQuotedлЎң мқёмҡ© span лӮҙмҡ©мқ„ кіөл°ұм№ҳнҷҳвҶ’мқёмҡ© н…ҚмҠӨнҠёлҠ” лӘ…л №м–ҙлЎң нҶ нҒ°нҷ” м•Ҳ лҗЁВ·мӢӨм ң bare invocationмқҖ unquotedлқј мң м§Җ. 13/13 verify(мӢӨм ң м°ЁлӢЁ мң м§ҖВ·мқёмҡ© mention нҶөкіјВ·mixedлҠ” мӢӨм ңкІғ м°ЁлӢЁ)В·tsc clean. force-push к°Җл“ңлҠ” мқҳлҸ„м Ғ stripQuotes(deliberate мқёмҡ© --force evasion нҸ¬м°©)м§Җл§Ң add-allмқҖ мӮ¬кі мҳҲл°© к°Җл“ңлқј мқёмҡ© лҜёкІҖмӮ¬к°Җ мҳімқҖ нҠёл Ҳмқҙл“ңмҳӨн”„. LESSON=к°Җл“ң нҶ нҒ¬лӮҳмқҙм ҖлҠ” quote-awareм—¬м•ј мқёмҡ© мқёмһҗ мҳӨнғҗ м—ҶмқҢ.

title: fix(pre): GIT-ADD-ALL-SHARED-MAIN вҖ” worktree-ліҙмң  mainм„ң all-form git add м°ЁлӢЁ(RC3В·нғҖм„ём…ҳ WIP мәЎмІҳ мӮ¬кі  мҳҲл°©)
body: RC3 shared-main mutate к°Җл“ң(мӢ к·ң modules/git-add-guard.ts detectAddAll + pre.ts л°°м„ ): worktree-ліҙмң  main worktreeм„ң all-form git add(-A/--all/./bare -u)лҘј nWt>1мқј л•Ң GIT-ADD-ALL-SHARED-MAIN full block. 2026-07-04 мӮ¬кі  мҳҲл°©=cd-into-removed-worktree нҸҙл°ұвҶ’mainм„ң git add -AвҶ’нғҖм„ём…ҳ WIP+.worktrees/* embedded мәЎмІҳ. detectAddAll м„ёк·ёлЁјнҠёawareВ·-C м¶”м¶ңВ·лӘ…мӢң/scoped/-p/--help нҶөкіјВ·14/14 verifyВ·tsc clean. config guardAddAllShared кё°ліё trueВ·no hatch. кё°мЎҙ 5к°Җл“ң(HEAD/ref) м»ӨлІ„л°– мқёлҚұмҠӨлІЎн„°.

title: changelog: CHANGELOG.d мЎ°к°Ғ-л””л үнҶ лҰ¬ м—”м§„ + gate adoption (conflict-0 В· auto-detect н•ҳмң„нҳёнҷҳ)
body: CHANGELOG.d мЎ°к°Ғ-л””л үнҶ лҰ¬ м—”м§„: add=мӢ к·ңнҢҢмқј1к°ң(unshift нҸҗкё°В·line-1 м¶©лҸҢ мҶҢл©ё)В·load=л Ҳкұ°мӢңjsonl+shard лі‘н•©(ts лӮҙлҰјм°ЁмҲңВ·malformed skip)В·prune/autoprune=мЎ°к°ҒнҢҢмқј keep-N unlinkВ·migrate --to-fragments(jsonl NмӨ„вҶ’N shardВ·undated=мҲңлІҲм—ӯмӮ° ts14). auto-detect(CHANGELOG.d м—Ҷмңјл©ҙ л Ҳкұ°мӢң 100

title: fix(git-guard): force-push нӣ… cross-segment нҶ нҒ° bleed мҳӨнғҗ вҖ” shell separator м„ёк·ёлЁјнҠё л¶„н• (м •мғҒ FF push мҳӨм°ЁлӢЁ м ңкұ°В·genuine force мң м§Җ)
body: RC1 force-push нӣ… cross-segment нҶ нҒ° bleed мҳӨнғҗ мҲҳм •(git-guard.ts): detectForcePushк°Җ shell separator(&& || ; | & |&)м—җм„ң нҶ нҒ° м„ёк·ёлЁјнҠё л¶„н• вҶ’force flag/refspecлҠ” мһҗкё° м„ёк·ёлЁјнҠёмқҳ git pushм—җл§Ң к·ҖмҶҚ. кё°мЎҙм—” gp+1л¶Җн„° мҠӨнҠёлҰјлҒқк№Ңм§Җ мҲңнҡҢн•ҙ `git push вҖҰ&& rm -f x`В·`push ; git push --force other` л“ұ ліөн•©лӘ…л №мқҳ нғҖ м„ёк·ёлЁјнҠё -f/--force/+refspecк°Җ мІ« pushм—җ мҳӨк·ҖмҶҚвҶ’м •мғҒ FF push мҳӨм°ЁлӢЁ. мқҙ м„ём…ҳ #4095 м •мғҒ pushк°Җ мқҙ лІ„к·ёлЎң м°ЁлӢЁлҗЁ. м„ёк·ёлЁјнҠёлі„ мҠӨмә”мқҙлқј genuine chained force-push(a && git push --force)лҠ” м—¬м „нһҲ м°ЁлӢЁВ·--force-with-lease/# force-ok ліҙмЎҙ. verify 11/11 pass(benign нҶөкіјВ·genuine м°ЁлӢЁ).

title: commons: +infra-wall-noneval вҖ” infra/toolchain/substrate walls quarantined from bench/experiment eval verdict (log as separate infra blocker + upstream-fix, never fold into target score В· clusters break-wallsВ·verdict-integrity)
feat(goal-guard): remnant scan catches 'мһ”мЎҙ' (formal synonym)
body: goal-guard remnant scan gains 'мһ”мЎҙ' alongside 'мһ”м—¬'/'лӮЁмқҖ' вҖ” the formal-register synonym for leftover work (e.g. 'мһ”мЎҙ мқҙмҠҲ 2кұҙмқҖ м¶”нӣ„ мІҳлҰ¬'). Same whole-message scan and the same 24-char negation window (м—Ҷ/0кұҙ/мҷ„лЈҢ-class past forms pass), so completion reports mentioning it as resolved stay unblocked. REMNANT_KEYWORDS list only вҖ” no logic change; tail defer catch untouched. QA: 8-case smoke via tsx вҖ” мһ”мЎҙ live 2 (м¶”нӣ„-мІҳлҰ¬ punt В· leftover-but-stopping), negated 3 (м—ҶмқҢ В· н•ҙмҶҢн–ҲмҠөлӢҲлӢӨ В· 0кұҙ), мһ”м—¬/лӮЁмқҖ regressions, defer-side regression вҖ” ALL PASS. ARCH module-goal-guard detail updated in place (284 chars, under cap).

title: fix(reap): MERGED-verify on local delete-branch failure (pr-cycle-ts-1 at a new call site)
body: reap misreported anima #2816/#2818 as 'merge pending' when both were already MERGED on GitHub вҖ” the recorded pr-cycle-ts-1 trap recurring at a NEW gh-pr-merge call site: gh's --delete-branch post-step fails on the LOCAL branch delete when a worktree holds that branch, after the remote squash-merge already succeeded. mergeStale() now routes both its plain and --admin attempts through mergedDespiteLocalError(): on a worktree/local-delete error signature, verify gh pr view state==MERGED and count it as success. Convergence record pr-cycle-ts-1 updated in the same commit (rule hardened: every NEW gh pr merge call site must carry a MERGED-verify). QA: help parse OK; the two live anima PRs verified MERGED (03963c2 В· c742daa).

title: feat(reap): checks-green --admin fallback вҖ” review-policy bypass with CI validation kept
body: Live-drain finding: both target repos have base-branch policies that a no-admin stale merge can never satisfy alone вҖ” anima requires 1 approving review (unsatisfiable on own PRs: you cannot approve your own), hexa-lang requires the selfhost-gates-summary check with enforce_admins on. New mergeStale(): plain no-admin merge first (respects every policy); if it fails on a policy/review error AND statusCheckRollup is all SUCCESS/NEUTRAL/SKIPPED (or the PR has no checks), retry with --admin вҖ” bypassing ONLY the review policy while keeping the CI-validation risk rule intact (Fable design: stale work never lands unverified). Applied to both the MERGEABLE path and the post-refresh merge. QA: help parse OK; validated on the live anima/hexa-lang drains immediately after shipping.

title: fix(reap): union-class delete/modify no longer blocks refresh (take base + lossless pointer)
body: Drain-blocker fix found live on the hexa-lang backlog (27 of 43 conflicting PRs blocked): union-class files (CHANGELOG/ING/unionPaths) hitting a delete/modify conflict were treated as code conflicts and aborted the refresh. Root case: hexa-lang migrated CHANGELOG.md вҶ’ CHANGELOG.jsonl, so main DELETED CHANGELOG.md while every stale branch still modifies it вҖ” stage :3: missing вҶ’ unionResolve bailed. Now unionResolve returns union|took-base|took-base-delete|null: base-side deleted вҶ’ git rm (follow the migration), branch-side deleted вҶ’ take base version; both non-union outcomes are reported via the existing dropped-files PR comment carrying the pre-refresh head sha, so branch-side content stays recoverable (no silent loss). QA: help parse OK; verified on the live drain right after shipping.

title: fix(pool): connect-only reach fallback вҖ” loaded hosts no longer mislabeled unreachable
body: Ports an UNMERGED improvement found live in the global CLI clone (~/.sidecar/cli had it as uncommitted local edits, blocking self-update вҖ” the fixed-but-unmerged anti-pattern; work preserved by porting to the canonical repo, patch applied 1:1). pool list/status live-load probe: a probe timeout no longer renders 'лҸ„лӢ¬ л¶Ҳк°Җ' unconditionally вҖ” on a null load a cheap connect-only ssh check (probeReach, 10s) distinguishes 'reachable but slow under heavy load' (new badge: л¶Җн•ҳ лҜёмғҒ(мқ‘лӢө лҠҗлҰј В· кі л¶Җн•ҳ)) from a genuinely unreachable host. Why: a host at load ~23 answers a bare ssh true well under the timeout while the heavy awk/nproc/nvidia-smi LOAD_PROBE blows past 15s, so loaded pool hosts were being mislabeled as down. QA: help parse OK; patch applies clean on main; ARCHITECTURE pool node updated (297 chars, under cap).

title: feat(reap): refresh-merge stale-PR drain engine + standalone cron-able command
body: New reap engine (modules/reap.ts) вҖ” gives the open-PR backlog an OUTFLOW. Diagnosis (2026-07-03, hexa-lang 46 open / 41 CONFLICTING В· anima 4/4 CONFLICTING): pr-cycle's happy path merges its own PR in-turn, but any PR whose session died first rots to CONFLICTING within hours because the doc-gate makes every PR touch the same SSOT hotspot files; the old reap only auto-merged MERGEABLE and merely warned on CONFLICTING вҶ’ zero outflow, monotone pile. Engine (oldest-first, own PRs only, per-run refresh cap): MERGEABLE вҶ’ no-admin squash-merge (stale work must pass checks; only the in-turn pr-cycle merge keeps --admin); CONFLICTING вҶ’ refresh-merge in a throwaway worktree вҖ” merge base INTO branch (squash makes the merge commit vanish вҶ’ plain push, force-push never needed), auto-resolving ONLY non-code files: CHANGELOG.jsonl/.md + ING.jsonl + config reap.unionPaths вҶ’ git merge-file --union (lossless keep-both), ARCHITECTURE.json/.md вҶ’ take base + PR comment with pre-refresh head sha (recoverable), reap.artifactPaths (default state/) вҶ’ take branch; ANY code conflict aborts untouched; shallow clones auto --unshallow on 'unrelated histories' (found live: hexa-lang is shallow, old merge-bases outside boundary). Backstop: code-conflict PRs inactive вүҘ reap.closeAfterDays (7d) close WITH BRANCH PRESERVED (reversible). Drafts skipped; external-author PRs never touched, surfaced as a count. Key fact documented: GitHub ignores .gitattributes merge drivers (incl. union) server-side вҖ” local refresh is the only automated path to clear CONFLICTING. Wiring: pr-cycle post-merge pass now calls reapStale(); standalone cron-able 'sidecar reap' [--dry-run|--max-refresh|--no-close|--artifact|--union] registered (cli + help + commands/reap.md); config lib/config.ts reap{maxRefreshPerRun,closeAfterDays,artifactPaths,unionPaths}; pagination limit 200 (was 50). QA: help parse OK; anima dry-run classified 4 PRs correctly (1 doc-only refreshable, 3 code-conflict with file lists); hexa-lang dry-run surfaced the shallow-clone wall which the auto-unshallow now clears.

title: feat(goal-guard): catch 'лӮЁмқҖ' (remnant) + 'к·ңлӘЁ' (tail excuse) keywords
body: goal-guard keyword expansion, mapped by catch shape: (1) remnant catch (whole-message, negation-aware) gains 'лӮЁмқҖ' alongside 'мһ”м—¬' вҖ” the plain-word synonym for leftover work; keywords generalized to a REMNANT_KEYWORDS list with the negation window offset keyed to keyword length. (2) tail defer catch gains 'к·ңлӘЁ' alongside rate-limit вҖ” closing on a scale excuse ('к·ңлӘЁк°Җ м»Өм„ң м—¬кё°к№Ңм§Җ') is a punt; size means keep going in chunks, not stop. Existing negation window (м—Ҷ/0кұҙ/мҷ„лЈҢ-class past within 24 chars) applies to the new remnant keyword unchanged. QA: 13-case smoke via tsx вҖ” к·ңлӘЁ tail positive/mid-message negative, лӮЁмқҖ live/negated(м—ҶмқҢВ·мІҳлҰ¬н–ҲВ·0кұҙ), plus regressions for rate-limitВ·лӢӨмқҢм„ём…ҳВ·мһ”м—¬ вҖ” ALL PASS. ARCH module-goal-guard detail updated in place (279 chars, under cap).

title: feat(load): open-PR count segment (wt вҶ” рҹ•җ) вҖ” gh via 5min TTL cache
body: load readout gains a PR segment between wt and рҹ•җ: this repo's open-PR count via gh, with lights рҹҹў0-2 рҹҹЎ3-9 рҹ”ҙвүҘ10 (same bands as wt вҖ” piling PRs violate cycle-docs-pr). The per-prompt hook must stay instant, so the one network axis is TTL-cached: gh pr list (--json number --jq length, 4s hard timeout) runs at most once per 5min, result stored in <git common dir>/sidecar-pr-count.json; within TTL every turn is a local file read. Fetch failure falls back to the last cached value; segment is omitted entirely when there is no repo, no gh, and no cache (line shape unchanged elsewhere). Legend line added to the inject body. QA: cold fetch 0.93s вҶ’ warm 0.41s (tsx startup baseline), cache file written with count+ts, non-repo cwd omits the segment, ARCH module-load detail updated in place (237 chars, under cap).

title: feat(goal-guard): DEFER_RE catches rate-limit excuses (EN+KO) as punt
body: goal-guard DEFER_RE now also catches rate-limit excuses in the closing tail вҖ” 'rate limit', 'rate-limit', 'ratelimit', and Korean 'л ҲмқҙнҠёлҰ¬л°Ӣ'/'л ҲмқҙнҠё лҰ¬л°Ӣ'. A rate limit is a wait, not a session boundary, so a reply that CLOSES on it is a punt and gets the same decision:block continuation push as next-session deferrals. Tail-only scope keeps mid-message mentions (e.g. a rate-limit issue already resolved) passing. QA: 8-case smoke via tsx вҖ” 5 rate-limit positives (EN/KO/hyphen/nospace), next-session positive, clean-close negative, mid-message-mention negative вҖ” ALL PASS. ARCHITECTURE goal-guard node updated in place (detail cell re-trimmed under the 300-char ARCH-BIG-CELL cap).

title: feat(accounts): inbox verb + pluggable postmark codeFetch source
body: inbox = one-shot verification-mail check across ALL roster accounts (age/n/code/from, --wait polls fresh arrivals). codeFetch now source-pluggable: gmail | postmark (Inbound Messages API, server token from secret, curl -K). Postmark scan merges processed+scheduled+failed status queues deduped by MessageID вҖ” the default listing hides webhook-pending mail. gmail API errors now exit 2 loudly. Live wiring: in.dancinlab.org MX -> Postmark inbound, roster claude{n}@in.dancinlab.org.

title: feat(pre): pr-cycle full-cycle rewriter вҖ” gh pr create auto-appends merge+cleanup (archive port)
body: PreToolUse(Bash) full-cycle rewriter for `gh pr create` вҖ” 1:1 port of archive_sidecar hooks/pr-cycle-hook/_pr_cycle.hexa into modules/pr-cycle-hook.ts, wired in pre bash (both CC and Pi call the same CLI). The command is rewritten in-flight (hookSpecificOutput updatedInput + permissionDecision:allow вҖ” allow is mandatory or CC ignores the rewrite) to append `&& gh pr merge "$(gh pr view вҖҰ --json number -q .number)" --squash --admin --delete-branch`, plus linked-worktree cleanup (cd main + worktree remove + branch -D) and a stacked --base вҶ’ default-branch override; --head/--repo pin the PR# lookup and route cross-repo merges. Deletion-sanity gate denies mass-deletion outliers (D>50 or D>=10xA with D>20, anima #1105 fingerprint) instead of auto-merging; --draft, explicit gh pr merge, and the __SIDECAR_PR_CYCLE__ mark skip. No opt-out by design. Smoke state/pr-cycle-hook-smoke/run.sh (11 PASS). Replaces the discarded goal-guard ask-detection approach вҖ” code-level auto-proceed, the agent never has to decide to merge.

title: feat(pre): GIT-EDIT-STALE-MAIN вҖ” block edits on a stale main when upstream already changes the file
body: GIT-EDIT-STALE-MAIN write-guard (pre write): DENY editing a file on the default branch of the main worktree when origin/<default> is ahead AND the upstream-only commits change that file вҖ” the stale-base duplicate-work trap (an SSOT section was re-authored on a 2-commit-stale main; the duplicate only surfaced as a pr-cycle conflict). TTL-fetched origin (git.staleFetchTtlSec, default 300s, attempt-stamped at <git-common-dir>/sidecar-fetch-stamp, offline degrades to local refs) so local refs can't mask a moved remote; merely-behind warns (GIT-STALE-MAIN); linked worktrees exempt; config git.guardStaleMainEdit default ON. Helpers ttlFetchOrigin/upstreamTouchesFile in modules/git-context.ts; smoke state/stale-main-edit-smoke/run.sh (7 PASS). Wired inside the existing pre write path so CC and Pi surfaces are both covered.

title: feat(accounts): multi-account roster over the secret store
body: list/status/code/add/retire/init вҖ” membership derived from secret-store keys matching group patterns (~/.sidecar/accounts.json = data, zero domain hardcoding), OTP fetch via gmail API (curl -K, creds from secret, --wait rejects stale), passwords never printed, legacy-namespace retire with dup guard, accounts.jsonl ledger

title: revert(worktree+load): back out #355 вҖ” gc scope + salvage + lab-wide wt gauge (redesign pending)
body: #355 м „л©ҙ мӣҗліө (мһ¬м„Өкі„ мҳҲм •) вҖ” worktree gc нҷ•мһҘ(м „ linked worktree reapВ·merged-dirty salvageВ·config worktree.salvageDir/labRoot) + load lab-wide wt кІҢмқҙм§Җ(wt N/lab M) лӘЁл‘җ лҗҳлҸҢлҰј. lab кІҢмқҙм§Җк°Җ л§Өн„ҙ readdir л№„мҡ©В·л¶Ҳн•„мҡ” л…ёмқҙмҰҲлЎң нҢҗм •, gc мҠӨмҪ”н”„ нҷ•мһҘлҸ„ мһ¬м„Өкі„ лҢҖмғҒмқҙлқј м»Өл°Ӣ лӢЁмң„лЎң revert. ARCHITECTURE.json мқҖ pre-#355 нҠёлҰ¬лЎң ліөмӣҗ нӣ„ #357 goal-guard л…ёл“ң к°ұмӢ л§Ң мһ¬м Ғмҡ©.

title: feat(goal-guard): 'мһ”м—¬' мһ”м—¬мһ‘м—… мәҗм№ҳ вҖ” лӮЁмқҖ мқј ліҙкі н•ҳкі  л©Ҳм¶”л©ҙ block, м§ҖкёҲ л§Ҳл¬ҙлҰ¬ мң лҸ„
body: goal-guard stop-check м—җ 2лІҲм§ё мәҗм№ҳ м¶”к°Җ: л§Ҳм§Җл§ү assistant лӢөліҖ м–ҙл””л“  'мһ”м—¬'(лӮЁмқҖ мһ‘м—…) к°Җ л¶Җм •нҳ• м—Ҷмқҙ м–ёкёүлҗҳл©ҙ decision:block мңјлЎң м§ҖкёҲ л§Ҳл¬ҙлҰ¬ мң лҸ„. л¶Җм •нҳ•-мқём§Җ(к°ҷмқҖ мӨ„ 24мһҗ м°Ҫ: м—Ҷ/0кұҙВ·к°ңВ·%/мҷ„лЈҢВ·л§Ҳл¬ҙлҰ¬В·мІҳлҰ¬В·н•ҙмҶҢ+кіјкұ°нҳ• вҶ’ live м•„лӢҳ) В· defer(кј¬лҰ¬ 8мӨ„)мҷҖ лӢ¬лҰ¬ м „мІҙ ліёл¬ё мҠӨмә”(мҡ”м•Ҫ мӨ‘к°„мқҳ мһ”м—¬ ліҙкі лҸ„ лҜёмҷ„лЈҢ мһ‘м—…мқҙлҜҖлЎң) В· кё°мЎҙ stop_hook_active лӢЁл°ң нғҲм¶ң кіөмң  вҶ’ м§„м§ң лё”лЎңм»ӨлҠ” мһ¬-stop нҶөкіј(session-terminal). л°°м„  ліҖкІҪ м—ҶмқҢ(лҸҷмқј нӣ… лӘ…л №) В· help/TOOLKIT/setup.ts мЈјм„қ lockstep В· QA 10мјҖмқҙмҠӨ(3 blockВ·7 pass) м „л¶Җ кё°лҢҖмқјм№ҳ

title: feat(goal-guard): also catch abbreviated multi-session punts (лӢӨм„ём…ҳ/лӢӨмӨ‘ м„ём…ҳ/л©ҖнӢ° м„ём…ҳ)
body: Broaden goal-guard's closing-deferral detection to the abbreviated multi-session forms вҖ” лӢӨм„ём…ҳ/лӢӨмӨ‘ м„ём…ҳ/л©ҖнӢ° м„ём…ҳ/м—¬лҹ¬ м„ём…ҳ/multi-session вҖ” in addition to the already-covered 'лӢӨмқҢ м„ём…ҳ'/next session family. This catches an agent wrapping up by punting remaining work to 'a separate/multi session' (the user's original 'лӢӨ м„ём…ҳ' keyword). Session-phrase-only additions (no bare лӢӨмқҢ/мһ”м—¬ words) so there is zero false-positive risk; still tail-precise + single-fire. QA: лӢӨм„ём…ҳ/лӢӨмӨ‘ м„ём…ҳ/л©ҖнӢ° м„ём…ҳ вҶ’ block; лӢӨмқҢ м„ём…ҳ regression вҶ’ block; 'лӢӨмқҢ лӢЁкі„лҘј м§ҖкёҲ м§„н–ү'/'м„ём…ҳ кҙҖлҰ¬ мҪ”л“ң лҰ¬нҢ©н„°' вҶ’ pass (no false positive).

title: feat(goal-guard): Stop gate blocking work punted to a 'next session' (revert #349 keyword rule)
body: New Stop-hook gate sidecar goal-guard stop-check вҖ” catches an agent PUNTING the work to a future session ('мқҙкұҙ лӢӨмқҢ м„ём…ҳм—җм„ң мқҙм–ҙк°ҖкІ мҠөлӢҲлӢӨ' / 'next session') instead of continuing now, and blocks that stop so the model presses on. This is the actual thing the goal-support request wanted (NOT the reverted #349 keyword rule, which is removed here). Scans the last assistant message's TAIL (last ~8 non-empty lines) for future-session deferral phrases (лӢӨмқҢ м„ём…ҳ/м¶”нӣ„ м„ём…ҳ/next|future|later|another session вҖҰ); a mid-message mention in analysis does not trip it. Emits decision:block with a reason telling the agent to continue now unless a CONCRETE blocker (external dep/CI wait/human approval/another machine) genuinely requires another session вҖ” in which case state the blocker and record the resume point via sidecar ing next, then stop (commons session-terminal stays valid). Single-fire via native stop_hook_active, so a genuine multi-session terminal that restates and re-stops passes through on the 2nd stop; correct operation never trips it (no Stop-hook-error noise). CC-only (Pi has no blocking Stop). Wired on BOTH surfaces: hooks/hooks.json Stop[] + modules/setup.ts Stop[] + install-hooks --global (verified live in ~/.claude/settings.json). QA: defer-in-tailвҶ’block, no-deferвҶ’pass, mid-mention-outside-tailвҶ’pass, blocker-1st-stopвҶ’block(nudge once), stop_hook_activeвҶ’pass, no-transcriptвҶ’pass, bad-verb exit1, help exit0.

title: feat fable-mode
body: fable-mode: DELEGATE лӘ©лЎқм—җ лӮңм ң(HARD PROBLEMS вҖ” л§үнһҢ л¬ём ңВ·мӢӨнҢЁн•ң мӢңлҸ„В·к№ҢлӢӨлЎңмҡҙ лІ„к·ё/мҰқлӘ…/м•Ңкі лҰ¬мҰҳ) м¶”к°Җ вҖ” directiveВ·on/status л©”мӢңм§ҖВ·ARCHITECTURE л…ёл“ң lockstep к°ұмӢ

title: feat(keywords): goal-continuity rule вҖ” route лӢӨмқҢ/мһ”м—¬/лӢӨм„ём…ҳ continuation keywords to /goal + ing (CC goal support)
body: Support Claude Code's native /goal feature via a keyword-router rule (design delegated to Fable 5 per fable-mode split policy; implemented locally). New config/keywords.json rule goal-continuity catches continuation-intent phrases вҖ” лӢӨмқҢ м§„н–ү/лӢӨмқҢ м„ём…ҳ/мһ”м—¬/лӮЁмқҖ мһ‘м—…/мқҙм–ҙм„ң/кі„мҶҚ м§„н–ү + next step/remaining work/keep going/what's next/continue from/\\bresumes?\\b вҖ” and its hint routes progress: single-session вҶ’ pull remaining from sidecar ing (next) or sidecar go, long-horizon вҶ’ native /goal <condition> or stop after N turns (auto-continues each turn, restored on --resume), multi-session wall вҶ’ sidecar ing next <resume-point> then STOP (session-terminal). NO new sidecar command: /goal state is CC-session-internal (a wrapper would be a blind echo = redundant-wrapper violation) and Pi has no /goal (agent-neutral). Role split from session-handoff: handoff=endingвҶ’record(write), goal-continuity=continuingвҶ’consume(read). Phrase-only patterns (no bare лӢӨмқҢ/next/continue) to avoid false positives. Data-only rule вҶ’ auto-applies on BOTH surfaces (CC UserPromptSubmit + Pi) via existing prompt-scan, zero engine diff, advisory (not per-turn inject budget). QA: 7 positive MATCH, 5 negative NOMATCH (лӢӨмқҢкіј к°ҷмқҙ/continue-on-error/next.js/кі„мҶҚ мӢӨнҢЁ/мһ”лң© all correctly skip).

title: feat(fable-mode): split policy вҖ” delegate DESIGN/ANALYSIS to Fable 5, implement LOCALLY
body: Rework fable-mode into a SPLIT delegation policy (per user: 'м„Өкі„В·л¶„м„қ л“ұл§Ң fable, мӢӨм ңм§„н–үмқҖ кё°ліём§„н–ү'). Previously ON delegated ALL substantive work (incl. implementation) to Fable 5; now the per-turn directive splits by KIND: DELEGATE the thinking (design В· architecture В· analysis В· root-cause investigation В· research В· review В· planning/spec) to Fable 5 via sidecar fable, but DO the implementation LOCALLY yourself вҖ” writing/editing code, builds/tests, git/worktree, commit, ship, and all repo bookkeeping (ARCHITECTURE/ING/CHANGELOG). Rationale: Fable 5's depth pays off on design/investigation, while headless delegation of code changes carries real friction (read-only default #347, context overhead, --json blindness, worktree stalls). Updated DIRECTIVE + on/status messages + module header + cli HELP + ARCHITECTURE node. OFF still emits nothing. QA: help parses, ON inject emits split directive (DELEGATE design + DO LOCALLY impl both present).

title: feat(fable): add --write (bypassPermissions) вҖ” headless fable is read-only by default; opt into full write/execute
body: New flag sidecar fable --write (aliases --bypass/--agent) вҖ” grants WRITE/EXECUTE by injecting --permission-mode bypassPermissions into the child claude -p (Write/Edit/Bash/git, no approval prompts). Root cause it fixes: headless claude -p runs in the default permission mode with no human to approve prompts, so Write/Edit/Bash/git calls are AUTO-DENIED вҖ” a delegated fable child is effectively READ-ONLY (Read/Grep/Glob + text .result) out of the box. This is why an 'implement X in a worktree' delegation reads+plans forever and never creates the worktree (git worktree add silently denied; --json hides the denial). --write makes fable a full unattended implementer. DEFAULT stays read-only (safe); write is opt-in per user request (лӘЁл‘җ н—Ҳмҡ©/bypass). Injected before o.extra so a user's own -- --permission-mode still wins (can narrow to acceptEdits or allowlist tools). --dry shows write=on(bypassPermissions)|write=off(read-only). QA: default no permission-mode, --writeвҶ’bypassPermissions, aliases, user override wins, regression 18/18 + edge 41/41.

title: feat(fable): add 'fable tail <id>' вҖ” follow a bg job's output stream live (watch progress)
body: New verb sidecar fable tail <id> вҖ” follows a background job's output stream LIVE until it finishes (the missing 'watch progress' view, alongside result/wait/list). Re-reads the small job out file each ~300ms and writes only the new tail; returns when the exitcode file appears, printing a done/exit trailer (exit = the job's code, 1 on no-such-job). A streaming non-json job fills out incrementally so you see it type; a --json job emits one blob at the end so tail stays quiet until done (launch without --json or with -- --output-format stream-json to watch it stream). Ctrl-C stops the VIEW not the detached job. 'watch' is an alias. QA: help parses, no-such-job exit1, live-follow of a real bg job prints incrementally then done trailer, re-tail of an already-done job dumps full out + trailer.
